name: Config Write Commands

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Config Write Commands is complete and correct

scope: Implement config set, config unset, config append, and config remove commands. 'config set key value --tier local' writes a key using dot-notation for nested paths; value must be valid JSON including arrays/objects, strings can be bare. 'config unset key --tier local' removes a key (writes null for deletion semantics). 'config append key value --tier local' appends a value to an array. 'config remove key value --tier local' removes a value from an array. Tier defaults to local. No array indexing supported. All commands validate the resulting config after writing.
success_criteria: All four config write commands (set, unset, append, remove) are implemented, registered, and pass comprehensive tests
Dot-notation path parsing handles nested maps, rejects array indexing, and validates path structure
Write operations use read-modify-write-validate flow with rollback on validation failure
Tests cover success paths, error conditions, tier targeting, validation rollback, and both output modes

breadcrumbs.text: Created 3 children in execution order: config-write-spec (spec), core-write-infrastructure (path utils + repository methods), cli-write-commands (4 cobra commands + tests). Ordering: spec first because it defines the contract; core infra second because CLI commands depend on path utilities and ApplyMutation; CLI commands last as the consumer of both.
